full_response:
We have to compute the Kretschmann scalar  

K ≡ R_{abcd}\,R^{abcd}

for the space–time whose Riemann tensor is supplied:

R_{abcd}= -\frac{1}{l^{2}}\left(g_{ac}g_{bd}-g_{ad}g_{bc}\right).          (1)

This is the standard form for a 4-dimensional maximally-symmetric space with
constant (negative) curvature k = –1/l².

Step 1:  Raise the indices on one Riemann tensor  
Using g^{ae}g^{bf}g^{cg}g^{dh} to raise the indices of the second factor,

R^{abcd}= g^{ae}g^{bf}g^{cg}g^{dh}R_{efgh}
        = -\frac{1}{l^{2}}\left(g^{ac}g^{bd}-g^{ad}g^{bc}\right).          (2)

Step 2:  Form the contraction
K = R_{abcd}R^{abcd}
  = \frac{1}{l^{4}}\,(g_{ac}g_{bd}-g_{ad}g_{bc})
                     (g^{ac}g^{bd}-g^{ad}g^{bc}).                       (3)

Step 3:  Evaluate the metric contractions in 4 dimensions (D = 4)
Expand the product:

(g_{ac}g_{bd})(g^{ac}g^{bd})          = g_{ac}g^{ac}\,g_{bd}g^{bd} = D·D = D²,
(g_{ac}g_{bd})(-g^{ad}g^{bc})         = -g_{ac}g^{ad}\,g_{bd}g^{bc}
                                       = -δ_{c}^{d}δ_{d}^{c} = -D,
(-g_{ad}g_{bc})(g^{ac}g^{bd})         = -D   (same as above),
(-g_{ad}g_{bc})(-g^{ad}g^{bc})        =  D².

Adding them,

(g_{ac}g_{bd}-g_{ad}g_{bc})(g^{ac}g^{bd}-g^{ad}g^{bc})
         = D² - 2D + D² = 2D(D-1).                                   (4)

Step 4:  Insert D = 4
K = (1/l^{4}) · 2·4·(4-1) = 24/l^{4}.                               (5)

Thus the Kretschmann scalar is a constant everywhere in this space-time.

\boxed{K = \dfrac{24}{l^{4}}}

Confidence: 93%
